{"step_by_step_thinking": "The child presents with features of sepsis, hypotension (BP of 90/60 mm Hg), tachycardia (Pulse rate 144/min), and tachypnea (respiratory rate of 30/min). These symptoms suggest septic shock. The initial management of septic shock includes rapid volume resuscitation. The Surviving Sepsis Campaign guidelines recommend a fluid challenge of 30 mL/kg of crystalloid fluids in the first hour. Normal saline (0.9%) is the most commonly used crystalloid for this purpose. Hydroxyethyl starch and 0.45% normal saline are not typically the first choice for initial resuscitation in septic shock. 10% dextrose is not indicated for initial fluid resuscitation as it does not provide the necessary volume expansion and may cause hyperglycemia.", "answer_choice": "D"}
